steps.title: Convert the time interval
steps.content:
$120$ seconds is $2$ minutes. Compare the population at times $m$ and $m+2$.
$$\frac{N(m+2)}{N(m)}=\frac{72Q^{\frac{m+2}{4}}}{72Q^{\frac{m}{4}}}$$

steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content:
When dividing powers with the same base, subtract the exponents.
$$Q^{\frac{m+2}{4}-\frac{m}{4}}=Q^{\frac{1}{2}}$$
So every $2$ minutes, the population is multiplied by $Q^{\frac{1}{2}}$.

steps.title: Convert to percent increase
steps.content:
A growth factor of $Q^{\frac{1}{2}}$ means the increase as a decimal is $Q^{\frac{1}{2}}-1$. As a percent, this is
$$100(Q^{\frac{1}{2}}-1)$$
The answer is C.
